entities.name: MICROORGANISMS
entities.description: Biological agents that play a critical role in nutrient transformation, soil health, and sustaining soil productivity

entities.name: NUTRIENT TRANSFORMATION
entities.description: The process of changing nutrients from one form to another, influenced by microorganisms

entities.name: SOIL HEALTH
entities.description: The state of soil functionality and sustainability influenced by microbial activity

entities.name: SOIL PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The capacity of soil to sustain crop growth, supported by microorganisms

entities.name: CROPPING
entities.description: Long-term agricultural practice studied for its effect on microbial communities

entities.name: FERTILIZATION
entities.description: The application of nutrients to soil, studied for its long-term effect on microbial communities

entities.name: MANURING
entities.description: The application of organic manure to soil, studied for its long-term effect on microbial communities

entities.name: RAINFED PRODUCTION SYSTEMS
entities.description: Agricultural systems relying on rainfall for water, used as the context for the experiments

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The climatic region where the long-term fertilizer experiments were conducted

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the research was conducted

entities.name: MICROBIAL POPULATION COUNTS
entities.description: The measured quantity of microorganisms in soil samples

entities.name: DILUTION PLATING
entities.description: The laboratory technique used to analyze microbial population counts

entities.name: SOIL TREATMENTS
entities.description: Different management practices applied to soil and compared with microbial counts

entities.name: SOIL TYPE
entities.description: The classification of soil used as a parameter for comparison with microbial counts

entities.name: SOIL MICROBIAL BIOMASS C
entities.description: The amount of carbon stored in the microbial biomass of the soil

entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC C
entities.description: The amount of organic carbon present in the soil

entities.name: RAINFALL
entities.description: The amount of precipitation, used as a parameter for comparison with microbial counts

entities.name: SOIL PH
entities.description: The acidity or alkalinity of the soil

entities.name: ORGANIC FERTILIZERS
entities.description: Nutrient inputs derived from organic sources

entities.name: INORGANIC FERTILIZERS
entities.description: Nutrient inputs derived from synthetic chemical sources

entities.name: VERTISOLS
entities.description: A type of clay-rich soil that showed larger organic carbon levels

entities.name: ALFISOLS
entities.description: A type of soil that showed lower organic carbon levels compared to Vertisols

entities.name: FUNGI
entities.description: Microorganisms whose population was higher in acidic soils and continuous inorganic fertilization

entities.name: BACTERIA
entities.description: Microorganisms whose population was higher in integrated organic and inorganic fertilization

entities.name: ACIDIC SOILS
entities.description: Soil with low pH that supports higher fungal populations

entities.name: CARBON SEQUESTRATION MANAGEMENT PRACTICES
entities.description: Practices identified to improve the capture and storage of atmospheric carbon in soil

relationships.description: increases counts when combined with inorganic fertilizers
increases counts when combined with organic fertilizers
supports higher population under continuous use
supports higher population in integrated use
supports higher population in integrated use
